comments.post_body:
EXCITING MILESTONE! 

I am thrilled to share that I have officially graduated with a Master’s degree in Fashion Management 🎓. 
This journey has been incredibly rewarding, filled with learning, challenges, and growth. I am deeply grateful to my parents, professors, mentors, peers, and everyone who has supported and guided me throughout this chapter, your encouragement and insights have been invaluable.
Over the course of my academic journey, I have had the opportunity to work on diverse projects in brand management, merchandising & buying, and marketing, collaborating with industry professionals and gaining hands-on experience in the dynamic world of fashion.

My key skills include:
 Brand & Product Management
 Trend Analysis & Market Research
 Luxury Fashion Merchandising
 Strategic Buying & Planning
 Creative Problem-Solving & Team Collaboration

Now I am actively seeking exciting opportunities in fashion buying, merchandising, brand management, and marketing where I can combine my creative vision with management skills to contribute meaningfully to the fashion industry.
If you know of any opportunities or would like to connect, please feel free to reach out, I would be happy to connect and explore ways to collaborate!
#FashionManagement #FashionBuying #Merchandising #BrandManagement #FashionCareers #FashionJobs #OpenToWork
comments.context_summary: The commenter is congratulating the post author on their Master's degree in Fashion Management and is likely responding to the author's announcement of their graduation and job search in the fashion industry. The commenter is showing support and possibly interest in the author's skills and job opportunities.

comments.post_body:
In my continuous learning journey, I am happy to share my recent achievements of MBA in Information Technology and Project Management with my LinkedIn connections. It was sheer a journey of two years, from January 2019 to May 2021. Time flies fast; after working full-time, I used to work on my assignments on weekdays, weekends occupied with research papers and project works, and twice a week - three to four hours of commute to school. It was challenging at first, but later on, I learn to manage my time wisely, prioritize tasks at best, and value time. 

I am thankful to Westcliff University, my employer ResMed for the support, and my Family for keeping faith in my perseverance in achieving my dreams.

P.S: I am so happy to enjoy my weekend back with no worries about completing an assignment. 

#MagnaCumLaude #learning #mba #projectmangement #informationtechnology  #womeninengineering  #warriors   #westcliff #university  #journey #perseverence #faith #dream #graduationceremony #pursuitofhappiness  #graduation2021 #continuouslearning #cumlaude 
#womeninbusiness #womeninstem #personaldeveloment #school #project #research #selfdevelopement #celebratingsuccess  #linkedin
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about achieving an MBA in Information Technology and Project Management, where the author shares their two-year journey of balancing work and studies, and expresses gratitude to their university, employer, and family for their support. The author is celebrating their graduation and reflecting on the importance of perseverance and time management in achieving their goals.

comments.comment_body: Congratulations Parth Pawar! Your perseverance and patience paid off. Welcome to Qualcomm 😀
comments.post_body:
I graduated in Feb'20 with a Master's degree from the University at Buffalo. Instantly felt I'm more than ready to rock the industry with a decent job in my domain!
The naiveness was short lived, as I was face-palmed with a global Pandemic around Mar'20 which affected the job search immensely. One by one, all my job interviews were either freezed or cancelled and there was a big question mark over an International Student's future in the country due to the 2020 political & medical scenarios.
In the meantime, one fine day, I saw my apartment complex burned into ashes during a fire accident. Everything I possesed, right from my immigration documents to my belongings were gone within hours and we were left homeless quite literally!
I was unemployed for over a year & half & was literally left with 15 days to fetch a job in the US to keep the American dream alive, or I would have had to pack my bags & leave the country forever till about last week!

Today, I have tears in my eyes when I write this post that I have accepted a job at Qualcomm as a Power Test Engineer. Things definitely didn't go as per plan, but now I feel it was definitely worth the wait.
I thank all those people who genuinely stuck around in these difficult times. You all know who you are, may God bless you!
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about overcoming adversity, where the author shares their challenging journey of finding a job in the US after graduating amidst the pandemic and personal setbacks, and ultimately landing a job at Qualcomm. The commenter is likely congratulating the author on their new job and expressing admiration for their perseverance.
